events.text: Q1 FY26: ~$1.494B of adjusted EBITDA, a record for a calendar Q1, +~20% YoY and +~85% vs Q1 2024. Generation $1.426B; Retail $68M (deliberately down YoY against a mild ERCOT winter). Fitch upgraded VST to investment grade in Q1, the 2nd agency at IG after S&P.

Management reaffirmed FY26 guidance for adjusted EBITDA and FCF-before-growth and held the FY27 EBITDA midpoint opportunity range — both EXCLUDE the pending Cogentrix deal and the Meta-PJM nuclear PPAs, to be updated post-close.

~$525M of buybacks in the first 4 months of FY26 + ~$75M dividend = ~$600M returned YTD; the 10b5-1 program accelerates on weakness. Line of sight to >$10B of cash generation 2026-2027: ~$3B to shareholders, ~$4B to accretive growth, ~$3B unallocated, at ~2.3x net debt/EBITDA by YE27.

Load growth 'real, but not at the extremely elevated levels on the fast timeline forecast by many third parties'; DC tightening only begins in 'late 2027 or early 2028.' ERCOT 5-6% and PJM 2-3% through 2030, but ERCOT forwards softened on mild weather and don't even reflect management's own 5-6% view.
BTM expert (ex-GE Vernova): behind-the-meter is a permanent solution, not a bridge — read-through to the grid-vs-BTM debate that frames the 'IPPs ceding DC share' bear.
Midstream DC expert: BTM at ~40% of power deals, with post-grid-connection BTM assets running only ~15-30% of capacity for peak-shaving.
MS constructive, highlighting the Helix Digital Infrastructure initiative ($10B, with KKR/NVDA/KIA) as a new theme, worth ~$23/share starting from an initial $1B.

JPM corroborates VST's marquee nuclear PPA — Vistra/Meta at Beaver Valley/Davis-Besse/Perry, 2.6 GW, 20 years (announced 1/9/26). Models 138 GW of US DC growth through 2030 with power the binding constraint; interconnection queues grow in ERCOT and Texas is a key BTM market given the lack of FERC oversight (a double-edged read).
